heading: Postal Service Health Benefits Program
label: 8903c Postal Service Health Benefits Program

L. 117–108, title I, § 101(c)136 Stat. 1137Apr. 6, 2022“(1).—DefinitionsIn this subsection, the terms ‘Medicare part A’, ‘Medicare part B’, ‘Office’, ‘Postal Service’, and ‘Postal Service annuitant’ have the meanings given those terms in , United States Code, as added by subsection (a).section 8903c of title 5“(2).—Information sharing by opmThe Office shall, by regulation, establish a process for providing such information as is necessary to the Social Security Administration regarding Postal Service annuitants (and the family members of such annuitants) who may be eligible to enroll under Medicare part B during the special enrollment period described in subsection () of section 1837 of the Social Security Act (), as added by subsection (b), or who may be subject to the enrollment requirements described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a).o42 U.S.C. 1395psection 8903c(e) of title 5“(3).—Information sharing by ssaThe Social Security Administration shall provide to the Office and the Postal Service information regarding whether a Postal Service annuitant, or a family member of such an annuitant, is entitled to benefits under Medicare part A and enrolled under Medicare part B, to assist the Office and the Postal Service in determining—“(A)which Postal Service annuitants, and family members of such annuitants, may be eligible to enroll under Medicare part B during the special enrollment period described in paragraph (2); and“(B)whether Postal Service annuitants, and family members of such annuitants, satisfy the enrollment requirements described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a).”section 8903c(e) of title 5

children.text: the term “covered Medicare individual” means an individual who is entitled to benefits under Medicare part A, but excluding an individual who is eligible to enroll under such part under section 1818 or 1818A of the Social Security Act (, 1395i–2a);42 U.S.C. 1395i–2
the term “initial contract year” means the contract year beginning in January of 2025;
the term “initial participating carrier” means a carrier that enters into a contract with the Office to participate in the Program during the initial contract year;
the term “Medicare part A” means part A of title XVIII of the Social Security Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 1395c
the term “Medicare part B” means part B of title XVIII of the Social Security Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 1395j
the term “Office” means the Office of Personnel Management;
the term “Postal Service” means the United States Postal Service;
the term “Postal Service annuitant” means an annuitant enrolled in a health benefits plan under this chapter whose Government contribution is required to be paid under section 8906(g)(2);
the term “Postal Service employee” means an employee of the Postal Service enrolled in a health benefits plan under this chapter whose Government contribution is paid by the Postal Service;

children.label: A
children.text: is a Postal Service annuitant; and

children.label: B
children.text: is a covered Medicare individual;
the term “Program” means the Postal Service Health Benefits Program established under subsection (c) within the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program;
the term “Program plan” means a health benefits plan offered under the Program; and

the definitions set forth in section 8901 shall apply, and for the purposes of applying such definitions in carrying out this section, a Postal Service employee and Postal Service annuitant shall be treated in the same manner as an employee and an annuitant (as those terms are defined in paragraphs (1) and (3), respectively, of section 8901), consistent with the requirements of this section.
apply to the initial contract year and each contract year thereafter; and
supersede any other provision of this chapter inconsistent with such requirements, as determined by the Office.

children.heading: Establishment of the Postal Service Health Benefits Program.—
children.label: c Establishment of the Postal Service Health Benefits Program.—

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: A .—Establishment
children.text: The Office shall establish the Postal Service Health Benefits Program within the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program under this chapter, under which the Office may contract with carriers to offer health benefits plans as described under this section.

children.heading: .—Applicability of chapter requirements to contracts
children.label: B .—Applicability of chapter requirements to contracts
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, any contract described in subparagraph (A) shall be consistent with the requirements of this chapter for contracts under section 8902 with carriers to offer health benefits plans other than under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to each plan provided by a carrier under this subchapter in which the total enrollment includes, in the contract year beginning in January 2023, 1,500 or more enrollees who are Postal Service employees or Postal Service annuitants, include a plan offered by that carrier with equivalent benefits and cost-sharing requirements as provided under paragraph (2), except that the Director of the Office may exempt any comprehensive medical plan from this requirement; and

children.label: II
children.text: include plans offered by any other carrier determined appropriate by the Office;

children.label: ii
children.text: provide for enrollment in Program plans of Postal Service employees and Postal Service annuitants, in accordance with subsection (d);

children.label: iii
children.text: provide for enrollment in a Program plan as an individual, for self plus one, or for self and family; and

children.label: iv
children.text: not provide for enrollment in a Program plan of an individual who is not a Postal Service employee or Postal Service annuitant (except as a member of family of such an employee or annuitant or as provided under paragraph (4)).

children.heading: .—Coverage with equivalent benefits and cost-sharing
children.label: 2 .—Coverage with equivalent benefits and cost-sharing
children.text: In the initial contract year, the Office shall ensure that each carrier participating in the Program provides under the Program plans offered by the carrier benefits and cost-sharing requirements that are equivalent to the benefits and cost-sharing requirements under the health benefits plans offered by the carrier under this chapter that are not Program plans, except that prescription drug benefits and cost-sharing requirements may differ between the Program plans and other health benefits plans offered by the carrier under this chapter to the extent needed to integrate the Medicare part D prescription drug benefits coverage required under subsection (h)(2).

children.heading: .—Applicability of federal employees health benefits program requirements
children.label: 3 .—Applicability of federal employees health benefits program requirements
children.text: Except as otherwise set forth in this section, the provisions of this chapter applicable to health benefits plans offered by carriers under section 8903 or 8903a shall apply to plans offered under the Program.

children.heading: .—Application of continuation coverage
children.label: 4 .—Application of continuation coverage
children.text: In accordance with rules established by the Office, section 8905a shall apply to health benefits plans offered under this section in the same manner as such section applies to other health benefits plans offered under this chapter.
shall be subject to the requirements of this section; and
may not enroll in any other health benefits plan offered under any other section of this chapter.

children.heading: Requirement of Medicare Enrollment for Certain Annuitants and Their Family Members.—
children.label: e Requirement of Medicare Enrollment for Certain Annuitants and Their Family Members.—

children.heading: .—Medicare covered annuitants
children.label: 1 .—Medicare covered annuitants
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (3), a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant may not enroll in a Program plan unless the annuitant is entitled to benefits under Medicare part A and enrolled in Medicare part B.

children.heading: .—Medicare covered family members
children.label: 2 .—Medicare covered family members
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (3), in the case of a Postal Service annuitant who is entitled to benefits under Medicare part A and required under this subsection to enroll in Medicare part B to enroll under the Program, if a member of family of such Postal Service annuitant is a covered Medicare individual, that member of family may not enroll under the Program as a member of family of the Postal Service annuitant unless that member of family is entitled to benefits under Medicare part A and enrolled in Medicare part B.

children.heading: .—Current postal service annuitants
children.label: i .—Current postal service annuitants
children.text: The individual, as of , is a Postal Service annuitant who is not both entitled to benefits under Medicare part A and enrolled in Medicare part B.January 1, 2025

children.heading: .—Current employees aged 64 and over
children.label: ii .—Current employees aged 64 and over
children.text: The individual, as of , is a Postal Service employee and is at least 64 years of age.January 1, 2025

children.heading: .—Postal service medicare covered annuitants and family members residing abroad
children.label: iii .—Postal service medicare covered annuitants and family members residing abroad
children.text: For any contract year with respect to which the individual is a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant or a member of family of a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant and resides outside the United States (which includes the States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Northern Mariana Islands), provided that the individual demonstrates such residency to the Postal Service in accordance with regulations issued by the Postal Service.

children.heading: .—Postal service medicare covered annuitants and family members enrolled under va coverage
children.label: iv .—Postal service medicare covered annuitants and family members enrolled under va coverage
I
children.text: is a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant or a member of family of a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant; and

children.label: II
children.text: is enrolled in health care benefits provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs under subchapter II of chapter 17 of title 38, United States Code.

children.heading: .—Postal service medicare covered annuitants and family members eligible for ihs health services
children.label: v .—Postal service medicare covered annuitants and family members eligible for ihs health services
I
children.text: is a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant or a member of family of a Postal Service Medicare covered annuitant; and

children.label: II
children.text: is eligible for health services from the Indian Health Service.

children.heading: .—Regulations for va and ihs exceptions
children.label: B .—Regulations for va and ihs exceptions
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section, the Office shall, in consultation with the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, the Secretary of Health and Human Services, and the Postmaster General, promulgate any regulations necessary to implement clauses (iv) and (v) of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—List of individuals residing abroad
children.label: C .—List of individuals residing abroad
children.text: The Postal Service shall provide a list of individuals who satisfy the exception under subparagraph (A)(iii) to the Office.

children.heading: .—Process for information collection and dissemination
children.label: 4 .—Process for information collection and dissemination
children.text: The Postal Service and the Office, in consultation with the Social Security Administration and the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, shall establish a process that will enable the Postal Service to timely inform Postal Service employees, Postal Service annuitants, and members of family of such employees and annuitants of the requirements described in paragraphs (1) and (2) in order to be eligible to enroll in Program plans under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “current option”, with respect to an individual, means the option under a plan under this chapter in which the individual is enrolled during the contract year preceding the initial contract year; and

children.label: B
children.text: the term “current plan”, with respect to an individual, means the plan under this chapter in which the individual is enrolled during the contract year preceding the initial contract year.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), in the case of an individual who is a Postal Service employee or Postal Service annuitant eligible to enroll in a Program plan under subsection (d), who is enrolled in a current plan, and who does not enroll in a Program plan during the open season that immediately precedes the initial contract year, the Office shall automatically enroll the individual, as of the start of the initial contract year, in a Program plan offered by the carrier of the individual’s current plan.

children.heading: .—Carriers offering multiple program plans or options
children.label: B .—Carriers offering multiple program plans or options
children.text: If the carrier of the current plan of an individual described in subparagraph (A) offers more than 1 Program plan or option, the Office, in carrying out subparagraph (A), shall automatically enroll the individual in the plan and option that provide coverage with equivalent benefits and cost sharing, as described in subsection (c)(2), to the individual’s current plan and current option.

children.heading: .—Carriers not offering program plans
children.label: C .—Carriers not offering program plans
children.text: If the carrier of the current plan of an individual described in subparagraph (A) does not offer a Program plan, the Office, in carrying out subparagraph (A), shall automatically enroll the individual in the lowest-cost nationwide plan option within the Program that is not a high deductible health plan and does not charge an association or membership fee.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section, the Director of the Office shall issue regulations to carry out this section.

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: 2 .—Consultation
children.text: In issuing regulations under paragraph (1), the Director of the Office shall consult, as necessary, with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, the Commissioner of Social Security, and the Postmaster General.

children.label: A
children.text: any provisions necessary to implement this section;

children.label: B
children.text: a process under which Postal Service annuitants and affected family members are timely informed of the enrollment requirements and may request, in writing, any additional enrollment information;

children.label: C
children.text: provisions under which a Postal Service employee or Postal Service annuitant enrolled under the Program may request a belated change of plan and may be prospectively enrolled in the plan of the employee’s or annuitant’s choice; and

children.label: D
children.text: provisions for individuals to cancel coverage under the Program in writing to the Postal Service because the individuals choose not to enroll in, or to disenroll from, Medicare part B.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Office shall require each Program plan to provide benefits for covered Medicare individuals pursuant to a coordination of benefits method approved by the Office.

children.label: A
children.text: a prescription drug plan (as defined in section 1860D–41(a)(14) of such Act); or

children.label: B
children.text: contracts between such a Program plan and PDP sponsor, as defined in section 1860D–41(a)(13) of such Act, of such a prescription drug plan.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (k), for purposes of applying section 8906(b) to the Postal Service, the weighted average shall be calculated in accordance with paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.label: A
children.text: enrollments for self only;

children.label: B
children.text: enrollments for self plus one; and

children.label: C
children.text: enrollments for self and family.

children.heading: .—Weighting in computing rates for initial contract year
children.label: 3 .—Weighting in computing rates for initial contract year
children.text: In determining such weighted average of the rates for the initial contract year, the Office shall take into account (for purposes of section 8906(a)(2)) the enrollment of Postal Service employees and annuitants in the health benefits plans offered by the initial participating carriers as of .March 31, 2023

children.heading: .—Payment of late enrollment penalties
children.label: 4 .—Payment of late enrollment penalties
children.text: The Postal Service may direct the Office to pay the amounts required by an agreement between the United States Postal Service and the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services under section 1839(e)(1) of the Social Security Act () from the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund established under section 8909a until depleted and thereafter shall pay such amounts from the Postal Service Fund established under .42 U.S.C. 1395r(e)(1)section 2003 of title 39

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Office shall ensure that each Program plan maintains separate reserves (including a separate contingency reserve) with respect to the enrollees in the Program plan in accordance with section 8909.

children.heading: .—Applicability of section 8909 to contingency reserves
children.label: B .—Applicability of section 8909 to contingency reserves
children.text: All provisions of section 8909 relating to contingency reserves shall apply to contingency reserves of Program plans in the same manner as to the contingency reserves of other plans under this chapter, except to the extent that such provisions are inconsistent with the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: .—References
children.label: C .—References
children.text: For purposes of the Program, each reference to “the Government” in section 8909 shall be deemed to be a reference to the Postal Service.

children.heading: .—Amounts to be credited
children.label: D .—Amounts to be credited
children.text: The reserves (including the separate contingency reserve) maintained for each Program plan shall be credited with a proportionate amount of the funds in the reserves for health benefits plans offered by the carrier.

children.heading: .—Discontinuation of program plan
children.label: 2 .—Discontinuation of program plan
children.text: In applying section 8909(e) relating to a Program plan that is discontinued, the Office shall credit the separate Postal Service contingency reserve maintained under paragraph (1) for that plan only to the separate Postal Service contingency reserves of the Program plans continuing under this chapter.

children.heading: .—No Effect on Existing Law
children.label: k .—No Effect on Existing Law
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as affecting regarding variations, additions, or substitutions to the provisions of this chapter.section 1005(f) of title 39

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: 1 .—Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “navigator” means an employee of the Postal Service or of a contractor of the Postal Service who is designated by the Postal Service or contractor to carry out activities under paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: 2 .—Establishment
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this section, the Postal Service shall establish a Health Benefits Education Program.

children.label: A
children.text: notify Postal Service annuitants and Postal Service employees about the Postal Service Health Benefits Program established under subsection (c)(1);

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the health care options available under such Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: the enrollment provisions of subsection (d); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the requirement that Postal Service annuitants and their family members be enrolled in Medicare under subsection (e);

children.label: C
children.text: respond and provide answers to any inquiry from such employees and annuitants about the Postal Service Health Benefits Program, in consultation with the Office as necessary;

children.label: D
children.text: in consultation with the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services and the Social Security Administration, provide information to individuals about enrollment under the Medicare program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act, and refer individuals to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services and the Social Security Administration as necessary for additional enrollment information; and

children.label: E
children.text: carry out, or provide for through contract or other arrangement, the activities described in paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—Information from opm
children.label: A .—Information from opm
children.text: The Office shall timely provide the Postal Service with such information as necessary to conduct the Health Benefits Education Program.

children.heading: .—Coordination with opm
children.label: B .—Coordination with opm
children.text: The Postal Service shall coordinate with the Office, in consultation with the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services and the Social Security Administration, to obtain and confirm the accuracy of information as the Postal Service determines to be necessary to conduct the Health Benefits Education Program.

children.label: i
children.text: Educational activities for annuitants and employees of the Postal Service to raise awareness of the availability of Program plans and requirements for enrolling in such plans, including requirements to be entitled to Medicare part A and enroll in Medicare part B.

children.label: ii
children.text: Distribution of fair and impartial information concerning enrollment in such plans.

children.label: iii
children.text: Facilitation of enrollment in such plans.

children.label: iv
children.text: Provision of information in a manner that is culturally and linguistically appropriate to the needs of the population being served by the Program plans.

children.label: I
children.text: engage in the navigator activities described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: avoid conflicts of interest.

children.label: I
children.text: be a health insurance carrier; or

children.label: II
children.text: receive any consideration directly or indirectly from any health insurance carrier in connection with the enrollment of any individual in a Program plan.

children.heading: .—Fair and impartial information and services
children.label: C .—Fair and impartial information and services
children.text: The Postal Service, in consultation as necessary with the Office and the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, shall develop standards to ensure that information made available by navigators under this paragraph is fair, accurate, and impartial.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this section, the Postmaster General shall issue regulations to establish the Health Benefits Education Program required under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: provisions for the notification of Postal Service annuitants and Postal Service employees about the Program, including a description of the available health benefits options, including a process for notifying Postal Service employees who become eligible for Medicare part B and Postal Service Medicare covered annuitants about their choices;

children.label: ii
children.text: provisions for notifying Postal Service annuitants, Postal Service employees, and their family members of the requirements under subsection (e) to enroll in Medicare as a condition of eligibility to enroll in the Program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a process, developed in consultation with the Social Security Administration, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, and the Office, for addressing any inquiry from Postal Service annuitants and Postal Service employees about the Program or Medicare enrollment.
